examples.title: Basic Process Termination with fork() and wait()
examples.description: Demonstrates creation of child process, child termination with exit status, and parent collecting status using wait()
examples.explanation: This program creates a child process using fork(). The child does some work and exits with status 42 using exit(). The parent calls wait() which blocks until the child terminates and collects the exit status. WIFEXITED checks if normal exit, WEXITSTATUS extracts the status. Since wait() is called, no zombie is created.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for parent after child completes

examples.title: Demonstrating Zombie Process (without wait)
examples.description: Shows how zombie process is created when parent doesn't call wait()
examples.explanation: This demonstrates zombie creation. Child exits immediately with exit(0), but parent does NOT call wait(). The child becomes a zombie - its process entry remains in the process table with its exit status until the parent eventually calls wait() or terminates. Run 'ps aux' to see the zombie process (state 'Z').
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - minimal, but zombie occupies PID table entry

examples.title: Signal Handling for Child Termination (SIGCHLD)
examples.description: Demonstrates asynchronous child termination handling using SIGCHLD signal
examples.explanation: This demonstrates proper asynchronous child handling. Instead of blocking with wait(), we install a SIGCHLD handler. When children terminate, the kernel sends SIGCHLD, the handler runs and reaps all terminated children using waitpid() with WNOHANG. This prevents zombie creation while allowing the parent to continue working.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of children

examples.title: Process Termination Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing kernel actions during process termination
examples.code:
// KERNEL ACTIONS DURING PROCESS TERMINATION

PROCEDURE terminate_process(process, exit_status)
BEGIN
 // 1. Change process state
 process.state = TERMINATED
 
 // 2. Save exit status
 process.exit_status = exit_status
 process.termination_signal = current_signal
 
 // 3. Close open file descriptors
 FOR each file_descriptor IN process.file_table
 DO
 close(file_descriptor)
 END FOR
 
 // 4. Release memory resources
 deallocate(process.page_table)
 deallocate(process.stack)
 deallocate(process_heap)
 
 // 5. Release CPU and remove from scheduler
 remove_from_ready_queue(process)
 remove_from_cpu(process)
 
 // 6. Notify parent (if waiting)
 IF process.parent IS waiting with wait()
 THEN
 wakeup(process.parent)
 copy_exit_status_to_parent(process)
 remove_process_table_entry(process)
 ELSE
 // Parent not waiting - become zombie
 process.state = ZOMBIE
 // PCB remains until parent calls wait()
 END IF
 
 // 7. Send SIGCHLD to parent
 IF process.parent exists
 THEN
 send_signal(process.parent, SIGCHLD)
 END IF
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the kernel's internal actions when a process terminates: state change, saving exit status, releasing all resources (memory, files, CPU), and either removing the PCB immediately if parent is waiting, or leaving it for zombie state. The SIGCHLD signal notifies the parent.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of open files/resources
